reasoning: The fast pace of direct negotiations between the US and Iran (four rounds within one month) strongly suggests a concentrated effort to reach an agreement. Israeli senior ministers being briefed that there is a 'high chance' of a deal further supports the likelihood of a breakthrough. With Trump's reported two-month deadline arriving in mid-May, the tight six-week window before the July 1, 2025 deadline will likely see the climax of these talks. Furthermore, the resolution criteria specify 'an agreement,' which would encompass an interim deal, a joint framework, or a freeze-for-freeze — all of which are significantly easier to conclude than a comprehensive treaty. Trump's history with North Korea demonstrates he is capable of moving quickly from threats to signing a high-level (even if less substantive) agreement. However, significant substantive differences remain (e.g., the US demanding total elimination of enrichment versus Iran proposing kept enrichment via a joint venture). Also, talks have experienced friction and delays due to new sanctions. Consequently, while momentum points heavily toward an announcement that halts the current escalation cycle, structural obstacles and the possibility of talks dragging out slightly past July keep the probability balanced around 55%.
batch_queries: Will the United States and Iran announce a new nuclear deal before July 1, 2025?
